title: DO NOT MERGE TEST ONLY MODIFIES VENDOR: Ignore EmptyLayer layers on storage input
body: TESTING ONLY this approach for fixing #684.

comments.body: Note to self: This may break existing installations - if the top layers are `EmptyLayer`, the topmost layer for an image will now be a the topmost non-empty one, and the code expects images with the same config to have the same topmost layer.  I.e. `pull foo; update c/image; pull foo` could break.
This is known to be insufficient for @pixdrift ’s reproducer from #684 , see #776 for the extra needed fix.
@mtrmac Since https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/776 has merged, can this move forward?

> @mtrmac Since #776 has merged, can this move forward?

It’s very likely this breaks corner cases (pulling the same image after upgrade, because the top layer ID changes; pulling v2s1 and pushing it out again, because the intermediate “throwaway” layers are not stored), I am still looking into that.

I’d also _very much_ appreciate a review by someone who understands c/storage and knows the context I don’t know I don’t know.
@nalind we need you to review this. ASAP

I might be off base, but thought this PR was not going to be merged.  Rather @mtrmac was going to open one in containers/image and then Buildah would have to vendor that in.  The changes s/b the same though, so a review would be good regardless.

> I might be off base, but thought this PR was not going to be merged. Rather @mtrmac was going to open one in containers/image and then Buildah would have to vendor that in.

Good point, yes, that’s the very likely outcome. But right now I don’t have a comprehensive c/image PR, so this is the only place I where can currently ask for a review of the core idea.

Please see https://github.com/containers/image/pull/472 for what could be a comprehensive fix. This PR now only vendors that branch to run `buildah` tests.

**NOTE**: That PR punts on handling pre-existing v2s1 images; users may have to delete/untag them and re-pull using the fixed code. Because comprehensively supporting the old representation would be a fair amount of complex code (and mean a commitment to add such support to any future layer-walking code), and because quite a few uses of v2s1 haven’t worked correctly anyway, on balance it seems to me that giving up is the better long-term choice.  OTOH possible deployments that only run containers (k8s nodes?), and are configured to re-pull the same image again for some reason, may be affected; please speak up in https://github.com/containers/image/pull/472 if this is not acceptable.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 733cd20) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
#860 is equivalent, closing this one to simplify the PR list.
